instruction: Classify the shipment incident report into an incident category.
baseline_prompt: You are a logistics incident classifier. Classify the shipment incident report into an incident category. Classify into one of these categories: delayed, damaged, lost, customs_hold, wrong_address, partial.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: The rail shipment that was supposed to reach the destination terminal on Monday is now showing an arrival date of Thursday due to track maintenance and rerouting through an alternate corridor.

Perfume bottles in this cosmetics shipment have leaked extensively due to broken caps and cracked glass. Strong odor pervades the entire delivery, and liquid has ruined labels and secondary packaging.
Overnight envelope was processed through our main sorting facility at 2 AM but has completely disappeared. Security footage review and facility-wide search have not recovered the item.
Freight detained at entry point because declared value discrepancy detected between shipping manifest and invoice. Importer must submit corrected paperwork to customs authority for evaluation.
Delivery attempted at commercial plaza address but suite number was illegible on documentation. Driver left package at suite 100 main entrance instead of suite 150 where the actual business operates.
Distribution center is reporting a shortage - they got 200 items instead of the 350 that were manifested. The truck arrived with several empty spaces.
Our same-day courier service failed to complete the pickup within the 2-hour window, and the package will now be delivered the following morning instead, missing the critical deadline.

The machinery parts arrived with severe impact damage to the wooden crate. Main housing unit has visible cracks, mounting brackets are bent, and protective coating is scraped off in multiple locations.
Container cargo inventory reveals 8 pallets are missing from the original manifest. These items were checked in at origin port but are nowhere to be found at the destination warehouse.
Cross-border transfer suspended at checkpoint due to missing end-user statement for dual-use technology items. Export control documentation must be furnished before transit can resume.

Recipient contacted support because their shipment ended up at their old residential location despite providing updated information three weeks prior to shipping date. The label printed with outdated details from our system.
Inventory check reveals we're short 75 units from today's inbound delivery. The BOL listed 300 units total but physical count only shows 225 pieces.
The cross-dock operation experienced significant backlog yesterday, and our shipment missed its outbound connection. It's now queued for the next available transport, which departs in 18 hours.

Forklift puncture observed on three pallets of canned goods. Several cases have dented and split containers leaking product. Approximately 15% of total shipment is unsaleable due to container compromise.
Tracking shows package was out for delivery yesterday morning, but courier confirms it was not on their vehicle. Item cannot be located at any depot or facility in the network.

test.query: Shipment has been sitting at the border inspection facility for 72 hours awaiting documentation verification. The broker indicates that additional commercial invoice details are required before release can be authorized.
Our driver delivered to the business address on file, however the customer had specifically requested delivery to their home. The special instructions were not reflected on the final waybill.
Driver left 12 cartons at the warehouse loading dock but the invoice shows 20 cartons were supposed to be delivered. We need to locate the missing 8 cartons immediately.
Air cargo flight was canceled due to mechanical problems and the freight was moved to the next available flight, resulting in a 24-hour setback from the original schedule.

The electronics shipment arrived with water infiltration throughout 6 of 8 cartons. Moisture damage is extensive - circuit boards are corroded and packaging is completely saturated. Filing claim for full replacement value.
Shipment appears to have vanished after arriving at the transfer hub in Memphis. No record of the package leaving the facility, and physical search of the premises has yielded no results.

Goods subject to extended examination at the customs warehouse. Random selection for physical inspection has added 5-7 business days to normal processing timeline while officers conduct thorough review.
The shipment arrived at the correct street name but wrong city entirely. Label showed Portland, Maine when it should have been Portland, Oregon. Sender provided ambiguous information during booking.
Our warehouse signed for 6 pallets but the purchase order specified 10 pallets of raw materials. The carrier claims everything was loaded. Need clarification on the discrepancy.

The freight forwarder informed us that the container will not make the scheduled vessel departure and will need to be rebooked on next week's sailing, pushing back the timeline by approximately 10 days.
Receiving dock reported that furniture items show significant scratches, gouges, and broken components. Two dining tables have cracked tops, and five chairs are missing legs or have splintered backs.
Express delivery parcel was loaded onto truck according to manifest, but driver claims it was not present during final delivery rounds. Package is unaccounted for and cannot be found.
Container remains in bonded warehouse pending importer's payment of assessed duties and fees. Release order cannot be issued until all charges are settled with the revenue authority.

Driver delivered the package to 123 Oak Street instead of 123 Oak Avenue as specified on the shipping label. Customer called stating they never received their order and neighbors at the incorrect location confirmed a package was left at their door yesterday.
Half of the pallet containing electronic components arrived today, but the other half with the power supplies was not on the truck. Please investigate why the shipment was split.

Driver reported mechanical issues with the delivery vehicle, causing all packages on board to miss their guaranteed delivery slots. Shipment is being transferred to another truck but won't arrive until tomorrow at the earliest.
Inspection reveals that 40% of the produce shipment shows bruising, soft spots, and early decay. Temperature and humidity controls appear to have malfunctioned, accelerating deterioration.

Package with tracking number XYZ789456 cannot be located in our system after the last scan at the regional distribution center on March 15th. Multiple facility searches have been conducted without success.

Package flagged during import screening due to incomplete harmonized tariff schedule information. Currently undergoing secondary examination at the international mail facility until proper codes are provided.
Parcel was left at house number 89 instead of 98 on Birch Lane. The numbers are similar and the driver misread the sequence. Homeowner at 89 brought the package to our depot the following morning.
Two of the three crates containing machine parts were delivered this morning. The third crate with the critical components is unaccounted for despite being on the same waybill.
Package was scheduled to arrive on Tuesday but is still in transit as of Friday morning. Tracking shows it's stuck at an intermediate sorting facility with no estimated departure time.
Heavy crushing to top tier of stacked freight has resulted in compromised product integrity. Cereal boxes are flattened, bags are burst, and loose product is scattered throughout the trailer.

Multiple attempts to trace shipment #GH-9087 have failed. The package has no tracking updates for 25 days since departing the origin city, and recipient confirms non-delivery. All transit points report no record of receiving it.
